title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Execution Trace: Feed Go Source Lifecycle
summary: Each user creates duplicate fetch jobs for the same feed, unfollow deletes shared data, ownership is ambiguous, and source verification happens outside a transaction. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go source lifecycle increment in shared-source and tenant subscription model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
